key_insight: A ~200 µm Al2O3–TiO2 D-gun coating substantially improves the wear resistance of Ti-6Al-4V, and random forest best predicts wear from sliding conditions.
engineering_relevance: This shows detonation-sprayed ceramic coatings can extend titanium alloy service life in high-wear aerospace, biomedical, and marine components.
method: Applied Al2O3–TiO2 coating to Ti-6Al-4V using detonation gun spraying under controlled gas flow and stand-off distance.
Ran ASTM G99 pin-on-disc wear tests at 40–100 N, 100–200 rpm, and 20–40 min against EN31 steel.
Trained ANN, KNN, and random forest models to predict wear from process/loading parameters.
evidence_signal: SEM and wear-test results showed reduced wear and protective tribo-layer formation on coated samples, while random forest achieved the lowest prediction error among the tested models.
watch_item: The study is based on a limited experimental parameter range, so model reliability may drop outside the tested loads, speeds, and coating conditions.
paper_title: Tribological enhancement of Al 2 O 3 -TiO 2 D-gun coated Ti-6Al-4V alloy: A machine learning approach
